sn41.1:1.0.1: On one occasion a number of elder bhikkhus were dwelling at Macchikasaṇḍa in the Wild Mango Grove.
sn41.1:1.0.2: Now on that occasion Citta the householder had arrived in Migapathaka on some business.sn.iv.282Then Citta the householder heard: "A number of elder bhikkhus, it is said, on returning from their alms round, had assembled in the pavilion after their meal and were sitting together when this conversation arose …
sn41.1:1.0.3: Then Citta the householder approached those elder bhikkhus, paid homage to them, sat down to one side, and said to them: "I have heard, venerable sirs, that when a number of elder bhikkhus were sitting together this conversation arose: ‘Friends, "the fetter" and "the things that fetter": are these things different in meaning and also different in phrasing, or are they one in meaning and different only in phrasing?’"
sn41.1:1.0.4: "Venerable sirs, ‘the fetter’ and ‘the things that fetter’ are different in meaning and also different in phrasing. I will give you a simile for this, since some wise people here understand the meaning of a statement by means of a simile.
